pagination.label: Notes Pages
pagination.focusHint: The current focus item is on page {{page}}, so the panel switched to the matching notes automatically.

home.title: Personalized Greeting
home.description: Adapts the greeting to the time of day and adds a short trend cue, such as steady improvement or a recent practice gap.

home.title: Weekly Stat Cards
home.description: Three cards summarize weekly sessions, average fillers per run, and change versus last week, with colors that reinforce performance at a glance.

home.title: Daily Filler Bar Chart
home.description: A quick weekly view of filler counts by day so users can see when they practiced and how each day went.

home.title: Daily Practice Tip
home.description: Turns recent practice history into one short suggestion, such as asking the user to watch their most repeated filler today.

home.title: Start Practice Button
home.description: The main call to action uses a subtle bounce so the tap feedback feels closer to an iOS control.

home.title: Quick Modes
home.description: Keeps the two most common follow-up actions near the top of the home screen so users can review the latest report or retry the latest issue without scrolling.

practice.title: Preflight Card
practice.description: Before practice begins, this card briefly summarizes the goal, preset, pace range, and retry focus, then hands off to the automatic 3-2-1 countdown.

practice.title: Recording Status Light
practice.description: A pulsing red dot signals active recording, turns gray when paused, and disappears after the session stops.

practice.title: Pace Gauge
practice.description: A live arc gauge shows speaking pace in wpm, with instant color changes for on pace, too fast, and too slow delivery.

practice.title: Live Stats Row
practice.description: Shows total fillers, the most repeated filler so far, and long pauses over two seconds. The counters react when a new issue is detected.

practice.title: Retry Segment Banner
practice.description: After selecting an issue in the report, this banner brings back the original snippet and retry prompt so the user can re-record the exact segment that needs work.

practice.title: Waveform Visualization
practice.description: Visualizes voice activity by expanding during speech and settling during pauses, making the speaking rhythm easier to feel.

practice.title: Live Transcript
practice.description: Transcript text appears in real time, with fillers highlighted and overly fast phrases marked directly in context.

practice.title: Recording Controls
practice.description: Three controls cover pause and resume, stop with report generation, and exit back to the Home screen.

practice.title: Goal Progress Card
practice.description: While recording, this card keeps the chosen practice goal and current progress visible so the user can tell in the moment whether they are moving toward the target instead of waiting for the final report.

report.title: Goal Result
report.description: The report opens by comparing the session against the single goal chosen before practice, keeping setup and review tightly connected.

report.title: Summary / Details Toggle
report.description: Lets the user stay in a quick summary view first, then expand the full charts, transcript, and exports only when needed.

report.title: One-Line Summary
report.description: Surfaces the single issue worth fixing first, such as the most repeated filler or a pace imbalance, so the user understands the top priority before reading the full report.

report.title: Top 3 Takeaways
report.description: This block surfaces the outcome, the main issue, and the next move first so the user can understand the report before reading every chart.

report.title: Quick Summary Card
report.description: In summary mode, this card tells the user to absorb the key takeaways first, then expand into the full analysis only when they need the score cards, charts, transcript, and share exports.

report.title: Three Score Cards
report.description: Three score cards condense the run into average pace, total fillers, and an overall fluency grade based on both filler frequency and pace quality.

report.title: Next 3 Actions
report.description: Instead of stopping at raw numbers, the report distills the session into the three fixes worth tackling first.

report.title: Filler Ranking
report.description: Ranks the most repeated fillers by count so the user can see which habit deserves attention first. Selecting a filler jumps through each occurrence in the transcript one by one.

report.title: Pace Curve
report.description: A line chart tracks pace across the whole session, with the recommended band highlighted and abnormal points emphasized for quick jumps.

report.title: Annotated Transcript
report.description: The full transcript highlights fillers and pace issues directly in context, making it easy to jump back into retry mode for the exact segment.

report.title: Share Card Preview
report.description: A dedicated preview card packages the score, top fillers, and pace rhythm into a fixed layout that works well in a README, portfolio, or hackathon recap post.

report.title: Share / Export
report.description: Alongside summary copy and JSON export, the report can export a dedicated share card as PNG or SVG. Demo mode only highlights the flow and does not trigger a real download.

report.title: Retry Result
report.description: When a report comes from a retry run, this card immediately tells the user whether the new take improved and offers a quick way to retry again or continue reading the report.

history.title: Overview Cards
history.description: Shows total sessions, total practice time, and the most repeated filler so long-term progress is readable at a glance.

history.title: Filler Trend Chart
history.description: Tracks filler counts across sessions so longer-term improvement is easy to spot.

history.title: Quick Compare
history.description: Two saved sessions can be compared side by side so users can immediately see changes in fillers, pace, duration, and their most repeated habit.

history.title: Session List
history.description: Each row shows the title, date, filler count, pace, duration, and fluency grade. Selecting a row opens the full report.

settings.title: Recommended / Advanced Toggle
settings.description: Splits the settings screen into a lighter recommended layer and a deeper advanced layer so first-time users see the most common controls first, then opt into finer tuning only when needed.

settings.title: Detection Toggles
settings.description: Lets the user enable filler detection, pace monitoring, and repeated-connector marking independently.

settings.title: Practice Presets
settings.description: Applies scenario presets that bundle a pace range with default filler categories. Manual edits switch the state back to Custom automatically.

settings.title: Practice Goal
settings.description: Lets the user pick one focus for the next session, such as reducing fillers or stabilizing pace. The same goal carries through practice and reporting.

settings.title: Filler List
settings.description: Default fillers and custom fillers are managed separately. Chips toggle enabled state, × removes an item, and Restore defaults only brings the built-in entries back.

settings.title: Pace Range Control
settings.description: Two sliders adjust the slow and fast thresholds. Changes immediately affect both the live pace gauge and the report curve.

settings.title: Feedback Mode
settings.description: Switches between haptics and sound cues for real-time alerts when a filler is detected.

settings.title: Site Language
settings.description: Switches the whole prototype between Traditional Chinese and English, including the in-phone UI, annotation cards, and guided demo text.
